purpose: Fixed five-unit development panel for theorem-level LaTeX-to-Lean context-selection and generation loops. This is a dev panel, not a held-out benchmark.
rationale: A single LaTeX theorem is useful only as a canary; it does not expose enough variation in source/project/local/Mathlib context needs.
The panel deliberately mixes known positives, unresolved context gaps, and hard dev-contaminated cases so each prompt or retrieval change can be checked against several failure modes at once.
Any theorem already used for manual prompt or hydrator iteration must be reported as dev-contaminated, not held out.

recommended_use.context_selection: Run all five unit ids in one selector batch when evaluating prompt/context-selection changes.
recommended_use.generation: Generate and verify per unit after hydration; compare success/failure taxonomy by panel role, not only aggregate pass rate.
recommended_use.benchmark_honesty: Do not promote this panel to held-out evaluation. Use it to debug loop mechanics, then validate changes on fresh sampled LaTeX theorem units.

units.why_in_panel: Short algebra theorem whose successful formalization depends on finding the right previous-project inverse theorem shape rather than broad Mathlib search.
units.expected_signal: A regression here means the loop lost a basic positive-control ability.

units.why_in_panel: A determinant theorem with local matrix style and standard Mathlib determinant APIs; useful for detecting whether selector/generator changes still handle ordinary Mathlib-backed statements.
units.expected_signal: A regression here points to local style/import or standard API context loss.

units.why_in_panel: Previously diagnosed as needing project/FPS context; tests whether selectors retrieve the local formal theory around formal power series division and coefficient congruence.
units.expected_signal: If selected context is insufficient, the bottleneck is project-local context retrieval rather than generation alone.

units.why_in_panel: The selector/hydrator can now expose checked Vandermonde and antidiagonal/range bridge facts, but recent generation still declined; useful for distinguishing Mathlib lookup from proof-planning failure.
units.expected_signal: If context is complete but generation declines, route to proof-planning or coding-agent diagnostics.

units.why_in_panel: The model can sketch zero-padding and inverse helper structure but has not completed Lean proofs honestly; useful for testing helper planning, no-placeholder normalization, and hard-case routing.
units.expected_signal: Repeated clean declines or placeholder skeletons indicate a need for stronger proof agents or manual helper-library growth, not more theorem-specific prompt hints.
